summary:
Perfect! The fix worked. To summarize what I did:

The problem was a circular import between `users.py` and `notifications.py`. Specifically:

1. `users.py` imported `NotificationService` from `notifications.py`
2. `notifications.py` tried to access `users.DEFAULT_ROLE` at the module level during import time

This created a circular dependency where each module needed the other to be fully imported before it could complete its own import process. 

I fixed this by replacing the module-level access to the users module with an inline string assignment in `notifications.py`, so it no longer references the `users` module at import time. Both modules can now be imported independently without creating a circular dependency.

The test now passes completely!
